blocks:
Ayahs

الٓمٓ (۱)
Alif, Lam, Meem. (1)
تَنزِيلُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ لَا رَيْبَ فِيهِ مِن رَّبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۲)
The [gradual] sending down of the Book, there is no doubt in it, is from the Lord of all the worlds. (2)

Key Points

Regarding the disconnected letters (حروف مقطّعه), we spoke in detail at the beginning of Surah Al-Baqarah; however, according to some narrations, the disconnected letters are among the Mutashabihat (ambiguous verses) whose knowledge is exclusive to Allah and His vicegerents (Awliya'). (345)

Question: Considering the doubt, skepticism, and accusations of slander and falsehood expressed by the opponents of the Quran—which some verses also allude to—how does this Ayah state: «لا ریب فیه» (there is no doubt in it)?

Answer: The Ayah states that there is no room for doubt or suspicion regarding the truthfulness of the Quran, its commands, and its teachings, not that no one has ever doubted or been skeptical about it. Therefore, in Ayah 23 of the same Surah Al-Baqarah, He says: «و ان كنتم فى ریب مما نزّلنا على عبدنا فأتوا بسورة من مثله» (And if you are in doubt concerning that which We have sent down upon Our servant, then produce a surah like it). If you are in doubt, bring a surah like one of the surahs of the Quran.

According to the author of Tafsir Fi Zilal al-Quran: An artificial flower does not become a natural flower, because the natural flower is itself a witness that there is no doubt about its naturalness. The Quran has repeatedly emphasized that it is from Allah, including:

«تنزیل الكتاب... من ربّ العالمین» «انّه لتنزیل ربّ العالمین» (346) «تنزیل العزیز الرّحیم» (347) «تنزیل الكتاب من اللّه العزیز الحكیم» (348) «تنزیل الكتاب من اللّه العزیز العلیم» (349) «تنزیل من الرّحمن الرّحیم» (350) «تنزیل من حكیم حمید» (351) «تنزیلاً ممّن خلق الارض والسّموات العُلى » (352)

Yes, the One who sent down the Quran is the One to Whom all existence belongs, Who has dominion over everything, Who is the All-Mighty (Aziz), the All-Merciful (Rahim), the All-Wise (Hakim), the All-Knowing (Alim), and the Praiseworthy (Hamid).

345) Tafasir Majma' al-Bayan and Nur al-Thaqalayn. 346) Ash-Shu'ara, 192. 347) Ya Sin, 5. 348) Az-Zumar, 1. 349) Ghafir, 2. 350) Fussilat, 2. 351) Fussilat, 42. 352) Ta Ha, 4.

Messages

1- The Quran took the form of a book during the very era of its revelation. «تنزیل الكتاب» (The sending down of the Book).

2- There is no room for doubt regarding the truthfulness of the Quran, because it is from the Lord of the worlds. «لاریب فیه من ربّ العالمین» (There is no doubt in it, from the Lord of the worlds).

3- The Lordship (Rububiyyah) of Allah necessitates the sending down of a firm and flawless Book. «تنزیل... من ربّ العالمین» (The sending down... from the Lord of the worlds).

4- The Book of Legislation (Tashri') is in harmony with the system of Creation (Takwin). «تنزیل الكتاب... من ربّ العالمین»

5- The revelation of the Book is for growth and perfection, specifically the growth and nurturing of all existence. «ربّ العالمین» (Lord of the worlds).

Explanations

Portrait of Surah As-Sajdah

This Surah has 30 Ayahs and was revealed in Mecca. This Surah is one of the 29 Surahs that begin with disconnected letters (Huroof Muqatta'ah), after which the Quran is glorified. It is one of the four Surahs in which an Ayah requiring obligatory prostration (Sajdah Wajibah) appears.

According to a Hadith mentioned in Majma' al-Bayan, the Noble Messenger صلى الله علیه وآله used to recite this Surah along with Surah Al-Mulk every night before sleeping.

Like other Meccan Surahs, this Surah also contains extensive discussions regarding the Origin (Mabda'), the Return (Ma'ad), and the fate of the disbelievers, in order to strengthen the faith of the believers and ensure their steadfastness against the pressure of the enemies.

In Ayah 15, bowing (Ruku') and prostration (Sajdah) during the night when people are asleep are mentioned as characteristics of true believers, and the recitation of this Ayah also requires an obligatory prostration.

Ayah

أَمْ يَقُولُونَ ٱفْتَرَىٰهُ ۚ بَلْ هُوَ ٱلْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّكَ لِتُنذِرَ قَوْمًا مَّآ أَتَىٰهُم مِّن نَّذِيرٍ مِّن قَبْلِكَ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَهْتَدُونَ (۳)
But they say, ‘He has fabricated it.’ No, it is the truth from your Lord, that you may warn a people to whom there did not come any warner before you, so that they may be guided [to the right path]. (3)

Key Points

Nadhīr (warner), derived from indhār (warning), refers to frightening accompanied by awareness, which is the same as giving a warning. Question: Although the Quran states: «و اِن من امّة الاّ خلا فیها نذیر»(353) (There was no nation but a warner had passed among them), meaning no nation was without a prophet, how does this Ayah state that no prophet had come to the people of Mecca before you? Answer: 1. Until the advent of Islam, the inhabitants of the Arabian Peninsula and the people of Mecca had not organized themselves and had not formed a unified nation (ummah); they lived scattered and dispersed. 2. The intention is that before you, no great Prophet who had a formal and public call (invitation) had come, not that absolutely no warner had come.

353) Fatir, 24.

Messages

1- Quoting religious doubts and false statements is permissible for the purpose of refuting them. «ام یقولون افتراه»

2- Denying the Quran is the constant method of the disbelievers. «ام یقولون» But you must state the truth and do not despair of their guidance. «لعلّهم یهتدون»

3- Disbelievers lack logic and resort to the weapon of slander (or accusation). «افتراه»

4- In the face of opponents' efforts to weaken the truth, the truth and the followers of the truth must be strengthened. «بل هو الحقّ»

5- The source of truth is Allah. «الحقّ من ربّك»

6- Allah has special favor toward the person of the Messenger (Rasūl Akram) صلى الله علیه وآله. «ربّك»

7- One of the main goals of the Prophets' mission (Bi'that) is to warn the people (indhār). «لتنذر»

8- Familiarity with the history of the audience is necessary for the preacher. «لتنذر قوماً ما أتاهم من نذیر من قبلك»

9- Warning (Indhār) is for the purpose of guidance. «لتنذر - لعلّهم یهتدون»

10- The Book and the Prophet are not sufficient for human guidance; it is the human being himself who must create the capacity and readiness for guidance within himself, and choose and select it. «لعلّهم یهتدون»

Ayah

ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا فِى سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍ ثُمَّ ٱسْتَوَىٰ عَلَى ٱلْعَرْشِ ۖ مَا لَكُم مِّن دُونِهِۦ مِن وَلِىٍّ وَلَا شَفِيعٍ ۚ أَفَلَا تَتَذَكَّرُونَ (٤)
It is Allah who created the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them in six days, then He settled on the Throne. You do not have besides Him any guardian or intercessor. Will you not then take admonition? (4)

Key Points

In the Arabic language, two words are used for "day": one is nahār (نهار) and the other is yawm (یوم). The first means day in contrast to night, while the second is also used for the totality of day and night, and for a period consisting of nights and days. Although Allah could have created the heavens and the earth in a single moment, He willed that the creation of beings should take place gradually. This Ayah states that the creation of the heavens and the earth took place over six periods (or stages).

Messages

1- To eliminate polytheism (shirk), one must speak of the eternal power of Allah and the creation of the heavens and the earth. «خلق - استوى - ما لكم من دونه من ولىّ»

2- The creation and the management of existence are inseparable. «خلق - استوى »

3- The Throne (‘Arsh) is the center for the planning and administration of the universe. «استوى على العرش»

4- One way of warning (or admonishing) is to present material and non-Divine supports and attachments as hollow and ineffective. «لتنذر... ما لكم من دونه»

5- One way of warning is reminding people of blessings. «خلق... أفلاتتذكّرون»

All these wondrous designs upon the doors and walls of existence, Whoever does not reflect upon them is merely a design upon the wall.

6- Man finds truths within his innate nature (fitrah), but he must be cleansed of heedlessness through admonition and remembrance. «تتذكّرون»

Ayah

يُدَبِّرُ ٱلْأَمْرَ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ إِلَى ٱلْأَرْضِ ثُمَّ يَعْرُجُ إِلَيْهِ فِى يَوْمٍ كَانَ مِقْدَارُهُۥٓ أَلْفَ سَنَةٍ مِّمَّا تَعُدُّونَ (٥)
He directs the command from the heaven to the earth; then it ascends toward Him in a day whose span is a thousand years by your reckoning. (5)

Key Points

| Question: In Ayah 4 of Surah Al-Ma'arij, we read: "The angels and the Spirit ascend to Him in a Day the measure of which is fifty thousand years." This Ayah [in Surah As-Sajdah] states that the measure of that Day is a thousand years? Answer: A: In a Hadith, we read: The Day of Judgment (Qiyamah) has fifty stations (Mawāqif), and each station is equivalent to a thousand worldly years, and the Ayah under discussion refers only to one station. (354) B: This Ayah concerns the ascent (urūj) of the command (amr), while that Ayah speaks of the ascent of the angels. Perhaps the ascent of the command occurs on a Day that is a thousand years long and is not the Day of Judgment, but the ascent of the angels occurs on the Day of Judgment, which is fifty thousand years long.

354) Tafsir Namūneh.

Messages

1- The center of management for the earth is the heaven; programs descend from there and ascend to there. «یدبّر الامر... یعرج الیه»

2- Monotheism in Lordship (Tawhid al-Rubūbiyyah) is inseparable from Monotheism in Creation (Tawhid al-Khāliqiyyah). «خلق السّموات و الارض - یدبّر الامر»

3- Divine planning (Tadbīr) is continuous, not temporary. «یدبّر الامر»

4- Just as in creation, everything is from Him and returns to Him, «انّا للّه و انا الیه راجعون», the planning of affairs is also from Him and returns to Him. «من السّماء - ثم یعرج»

Ayahs

ذَٰلِكَ عَـٰلِمُ ٱلْغَيْبِ وَٱلشَّهَـٰدَةِ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ (٦)
That is the Knower of the sensible and the Unseen, the All-mighty, the All-merciful, (6)
ٱلَّذِىٓ أَحْسَنَ كُلَّ شَىْءٍ خَلَقَهُۥ ۖ وَبَدَأَ خَلْقَ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنِ مِن طِينٍ (۷)
who perfected everything that He created and commenced man’s creation from clay. (7)
ثُمَّ جَعَلَ نَسْلَهُۥ مِن سُلَـٰلَةٍ مِّن مَّآءٍ مَّهِينٍ (۸)
Then He made his progeny from an extract of a base fluid. (8)
ثُمَّ سَوَّىٰهُ وَنَفَخَ فِيهِ مِن رُّوحِهِۦ ۖ وَجَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلسَّمْعَ وَٱلْأَبْصَـٰرَ وَٱلْأَفْـِٔدَةَ ۚ قَلِيلًا مَّا تَشْكُرُونَ (۹)
Then He proportioned him and breathed into him of His Spirit, and made for you hearing, sight, and hearts. Little do you thank. (9)

Key Points

The word sawwā (سَوّى) from taswiyah (تسویه) means establishing balance and proportion, and avoiding any form of excess or deficiency (extremism). The realities of the world are of two types for us: manifest and hidden; but nothing is absent or concealed from Allah.

Messages

1- The laws governing the universe are based on Allah's infinite knowledge. «يدبّر الأمر... عالم الغيب والشهادة» (Indeed, the planning and administration of any system requires knowledge of it.)

2- Divine knowledge is uniform regarding the manifest and the hidden. «عالم الغيب والشهادة»

3- Divine power is accompanied by mercy. «العزيز الرحيم»

4- All creation is good. «أحسن كلّ شيء خلقه» (Even venom in a snake's body is a value, just as saliva is a blessing inside a human's mouth; but if it exits and falls anywhere, it is an insult/disgrace.)

5- Everything is created by Allah. «كلّ شيء خلقه»

6- The separate mention of humanity, alongside all existence, indicates the special importance and value of humankind. «كلّ شيء خلقه - خلق الإنسان»

7- What becomes semen (nutfah) is nothing more than a single cell and sperm. «سلالة من ماء»

8- The Artist is Allah, Who creates a noble and valuable being from a mere and lowly drop of water. «خلق... من ماء مهين»

9- The creation of Prophet Adam (عليه السلام) is different from the creation of the children of Adam. «بدأ خلق الإنسان من طين ثمّ جعل نسله من سلالة من ماء مهين» (The creation of Prophet Adam was from clay, but his progeny was created from semen and water.)

10- Receiving Divine perfections requires readiness and balance. (First, a balanced physique, then the blowing of the Divine Spirit.) «ثم سوّاه ونفخ فيه»

11- One of the signs of human nobility is the blowing of the Divine Spirit into him. «من روحه»

12- The creation of the first human was gradual. «بدأ خلق الإنسان... ثمّ سوّاه»

13- The scope of human sight is greater than that of his hearing. (A person cannot hear two separate sounds distinctly at the same moment, but he can observe several things simultaneously. Furthermore, through the ear, a person only hears the sound of objects and individuals, but through the eye, he sees position, color, volume, movement, and stillness.) («السمع» [hearing] is singular and «الأبصار» [sights] is plural.)

14- Among the body's organs, those that are means of recognition and knowledge are the most important. (Therefore, only the ear, the eye, and the heart/intellect are mentioned.) «السمع والأبصار والأفئدة»

15- Attention to the stages of human creation is a path toward self-knowledge, knowledge of Allah, and gratitude. «قليلاً ما تشكرون»

16- Humanity must be grateful, otherwise it deserves reproach. «قليلاً ما تشكرون»

Ayah

وَقَالُوٓا۟ أَءِذَا ضَلَلْنَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ أَءِنَّا لَفِى خَلْقٍ جَدِيدٍۭ ۚ بَلْ هُم بِلِقَآءِ رَبِّهِمْ كَـٰفِرُونَ (۱٠)
They say, ‘When we have been lost in the dust, shall we be indeed created anew?’ Indeed, they disbelieve in the encounter with their Lord. (10)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, we read that Allah gave you the means of perception and understanding (eyes, ears, and heart), and then complained that you show little gratitude. This Ayah states one example of ingratitude: that despite witnessing all this display of Allah's power, man still doubts the establishment of the Resurrection (Qiyamah).

Messages

1- By citing the doubts of the opponents and providing decisive answers to them, we should insure and vaccinate the believers. «قالوا...»

2- What the deniers of the Resurrection doubted was the physical resurrection (Ma'ad Jismānī). «ضللنا فى الارض...»

3- Resolve your doubts and uncertainties with clear evidence, and do not let them lead to destruction. «ءَ اذا... بل هم بلقاء ربّهم كافرون»

4- The disbelievers (Kuffār) are also under the management (Tadbīr) of Allah's Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «بلقاء ربّهم كافرون»

Ayah

۞ قُلْ يَتَوَفَّىٰكُم مَّلَكُ ٱلْمَوْتِ ٱلَّذِى وُكِّلَ بِكُمْ ثُمَّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكُمْ تُرْجَعُونَ (۱۱)
Say, ‘You will be taken away by the angel of death, who has been charged with you. Then you will be brought back to your Lord.’ (11)

Key Points

The word tawaffā (تَوَفّى) means the complete retrieval of something. Considering that the body of the deceased remains in its place after death, the intention of "complete retrieval" is the retrieval of the soul, which is the reality of the human being. If the soul departs, the human being has completely departed. Therefore, the soul possesses originality and independence, and it remains. In the Hereafter, this very soul will be resurrected by rejoining the body.

The Qur'an uses three expressions regarding the method of taking the soul (qabḍ ar-rūḥ): A: In one place, He says: The angels take the life and soul of man. «تتوفّاهم الملائكة» (355) B: In the verse under discussion, the agent responsible for taking the soul is introduced as an angel named the Angel of Death (Malak al-Mawt). C: In Ayah 42 of Surah Az-Zumar, this action is attributed to Allah. «اللّه یتوفى الانفس»

In reconciling these three verses, perhaps it can be said: The angels take the soul and hand it over to the Angel of Death, and he hands it over to God Almighty. Just as Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, in response to the question of how the Angel of Death can be present everywhere at the same moment when many people die simultaneously across the world, said: "He has helpers among the angels who take the souls of people from various places, and then the Angel of Death receives the souls from the angels." (356)

Perhaps the ranks of people also cause a difference in how their souls are taken, meaning that the souls of ordinary people are taken by the angels, the souls of the pious are taken by the Angel of Death, and the souls of the friends of Allah are taken by Allah Himself.

355) An-Nahl, 28. 356) Tafsir Nur Ath-Thaqalayn.

Messages

  1. Do not leave doubts unanswered. «قالوا... قل...»
  2. The reality of man is his soul; as soon as the soul is taken, it is as if the man himself has been taken. «یتوفّاكم»
  3. Angels do not shirk their duty. «ملك الموت الّذى وُكّل بكم»
  4. The death and burial of man is a step toward his revival, just as burying a seed is a step toward its sprouting. «ثم الى ربّكم ترجعون»
  5. The same God who takes the souls will one day, by a single Will, return all human beings to Himself. «ترجعون»

Ayah

وَلَوْ تَرَىٰٓ إِذِ ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ نَاكِسُوا۟ رُءُوسِهِمْ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ رَبَّنَآ أَبْصَرْنَا وَسَمِعْنَا فَٱرْجِعْنَا نَعْمَلْ صَـٰلِحًا إِنَّا مُوقِنُونَ (۱۲)
Were you to see when the guilty hang their heads before their Lord [confessing], ‘Our Lord! We have seen and heard. Send us back so that we may act righteously. Indeed we are [now] convinced.’ (12)

Key Points

Although in this Ayah, the criminals request a return to the world to perform righteous deeds, as we read in Ayah 28 of Surah Al-An'am, they would still transgress if they were returned to the world. «ولو رُدّوا لعادوا لمانُهوا عنه و انّهم لكاذبون»

Messages

1- The rebellion of the criminals today leads to the humiliation of the Day of Judgment tomorrow, and this is a source of solace for the Prophet and the believers. «ولو ترى »

2- The Day of Judgment is the day of humiliation and shame for the criminals. «المجرمون ناكسوا رؤسهم»

3- The Day of Judgment is the day of confession. «ربّنا ابصرنا و سمعنا»

4- The Day of Judgment is the day of the unveiling of truths and the opening of eyes and ears. (Those who had closed their eyes and ears to seeing and hearing the truth will say on that day: «ابصرنا و سمعنا»)

5- What causes salvation on the Day of Judgment is only righteous deeds, the place for which is this world. «فارجعنا نعمل صالحاً»

6- The reason for crime and sin is the lack of belief in the Day of Judgment. (They will say there: «انّا موقنون», but what good will that do?)

Ayah

وَلَوْ شِئْنَا لَـَٔاتَيْنَا كُلَّ نَفْسٍ هُدَىٰهَا وَلَـٰكِنْ حَقَّ ٱلْقَوْلُ مِنِّى لَأَمْلَأَنَّ جَهَنَّمَ مِنَ ٱلْجِنَّةِ وَٱلنَّاسِ أَجْمَعِينَ (۱۳)
Had We wished We would have given every soul its guidance, but My word became due [against the faithless]: ‘Surely I will fill hell with all the [guilty] jinn and humans.’ (13)

Messages

1- Divine actions are dependent on His Will and intention. «ولوشئنا لآتینا...»

2- Accepting guidance must be voluntary, not compulsory. «ولوشئنا لآتینا...»

3- People are diverse, and every soul has guidance specific to itself. «كلّ نفسٍ هُداها»

4- The vast Divine Mercy does not prevent His wrath. «لَاَملأن»

5- Jinn, like humans, are subject to Divine obligation, favor, and wrath. «من الجنة والنّاس»

Ayah

فَذُوقُوا۟ بِمَا نَسِيتُمْ لِقَآءَ يَوْمِكُمْ هَـٰذَآ إِنَّا نَسِينَـٰكُمْ ۖ وَذُوقُوا۟ عَذَابَ ٱلْخُلْدِ بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ (۱٤)
So taste [the punishment] for your having forgotten the encounter of this day of yours. We [too] have forgotten you. Taste the everlasting punishment because of what you used to do. (14)

Key Points

In the two preceding verses, the criminals requested a return to the world. Perhaps this verse is a rejection of their request. Attributing forgetfulness (nissyan) to Allah is meaningless. Therefore, the intent of "نسینا" (We forgot) is that Our dealing with you, who forgot the Day of Judgment, is the dealing of one who has consigned you to oblivion.

Messages

1- Explain the reason for the punishment to the criminals. «فذوقوا بما نسیتم»

2- Divine punishments are just and commensurate (tit-for-tat). «نسیتم - نسینا»

3- Remembering the Resurrection (Ma'ad) is the key to salvation, and heedlessness concerning it is the cause of distress. «بما نسیتم»

4- The source of corrupt deeds is heedlessness concerning the Resurrection. «نسیتم - كنتم تعملون»

5- The continuation of evil deeds leads to permanence in the torment of Hell. «عذاب الخلد بما كنتم تعملون»

6- Every stance and action has a punishment appropriate to it. (The repetition of "ذوقوا" [taste] indicates that forgetting the Day of Judgment has one torment, and man's actions have another torment.)

Ayah

إِنَّمَا يُؤْمِنُ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا ٱلَّذِينَ إِذَا ذُكِّرُوا۟ بِهَا خَرُّوا۟ سُجَّدًا وَسَبَّحُوا۟ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّهِمْ وَهُمْ لَا يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ ۩ (۱٥)
Only those believe in Our signs who, when they are reminded of them, fall down in prostration and celebrate the praise of their Lord, and they are not arrogant. (15)

Key Points

In four chapters (Surahs) of the Quran, we encounter verses whose recitation necessitates prostration (Sajdah). These Surahs are: Fussilat, Najm, 'Alaq, and Sajdah, which are technically named the Surahs of 'Aza'im (the Obligatory Prostrations). According to the school of the Ahl al-Bayt (عليه السلام), reciting these four Surahs in prayer after Surah al-Hamd is not permissible, and those who are in a state of major ritual impurity (Junub) or menstruation (Ha'idh) must not recite even a single verse from these four Surahs.

Messages

  1. Loving prostration, glorification (Tasbih), and praise of Allah are signs of the believer. «انّما یؤمن»

  2. In its method of propagation and guidance, the Quran, alongside depicting the characteristics of criminals, also describes the signs of believers. «الّذین اذا ذكروا خرّوا...»

  3. The withholding of divine guidance from a group of people is due to their own arrogance. «لو شئنا لآتینا كلّ نفس هداها... انّما یؤمن... الّذین... هم لایستكبرون»

  4. Divine verses are in harmony with human nature (Fitrah) and intellect, and only require reminder. «اذا ذكّروا...»

  5. The prostrations of believers are not exclusive to the time of prayer. «اذا ذكّروا... خرّوا»

  6. In worship, time is relevant, «اذا» (when); the context is relevant, «ذكّروا» (they are reminded); and the action is relevant. «خرّوا» (they fall down).

  7. Prostration must be loving. «خرّوا سجّدا» It is as if they fall to the ground with their entire being.

  8. The best remembrance (Dhikr) during prostration is glorification accompanied by praise. «سجّداً و سبّحوا بحمد ربّهم»

  9. A prostration is valuable only if it is not followed by pride and arrogance. «خرّوا سجّداً... و هم لایستكبرون»

Ayah

تَتَجَافَىٰ جُنُوبُهُمْ عَنِ ٱلْمَضَاجِعِ يَدْعُونَ رَبَّهُمْ خَوْفًا وَطَمَعًا وَمِمَّا رَزَقْنَـٰهُمْ يُنفِقُونَ (۱٦)
Their sides vacate their beds to supplicate their Lord in fear and hope, and they spend out of what We have provided them. (16)

Messages

1- Early rising and detaching oneself from the bed is another sign of the believers. «تتجافى جنوبهم...»

2- Acts of worship that are accompanied by a degree of self-discipline and privacy have greater value. «تتجافى»

3- The believer is neither despairing nor arrogant; he has both fear and hope. «یدعون ربّهم خوفاً و طمعاً»

4- Humility, early rising, and spending (in charity) are permanent characteristics of the believers. «لایستكبرون - یدعون - ینفقون» (All are presented in the present tense form.)

5- The nightly connection with Allah must be accompanied by attending to the deprived. «یدعون - ینفقون»

6- The object of spending is not limited to wealth, but also includes knowledge, honor, and other human assets. «ممّا رزقناهم ینفقون»

Ayah

فَلَا تَعْلَمُ نَفْسٌ مَّآ أُخْفِىَ لَهُم مِّن قُرَّةِ أَعْيُنٍ جَزَآءًۢ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (۱۷)
No one knows what delights have been kept hidden for them [in the Hereafter] as a reward for what they used to do. (17)

Key Points

The Quran uses several expressions regarding the measure of divine rewards. In one place, it uses "ضِعف" (357) meaning double; in another, "اَضعافاً" (358) meaning manifold (several times over); in another, "عشر امثالها" (359) meaning ten times; and in yet another place, it is likened to a seed that produces seven ears, with a hundred grains in every ear, meaning seven hundred times; (360) But this verse speaks of something beyond numbers and figures, stating: No one is aware of the reward for this group of believers, who are devoted to the night prayer (Salat al-Layl) and charity (Infaq). Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: "The reward for every good deed has been mentioned in the Quran, except for the night prayer; no one knows its reward except Allah. Then He recited this verse." (361)

357) Al-A'raf, 38. 358) Al-Baqarah, 245. 359) Al-An'am, 160. 360) Al-Baqarah, 261. 361) Tafasir Majma' al-Bayan and Al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Waking up before dawn (Sahar-khizi), the night prayer, and helping the deprived yield the highest rewards. «فلا تعلم نفس»

2- Some divine rewards are incomprehensible to us. «فلا تعلم نفس»

3- In the method of upbringing (Tarbiya), sometimes the type and amount of encouragement and reward must be kept hidden. «ما اُخفى لهم»

4- Hidden worship yields hidden rewards. «تتجافى... اُخفى لهم»

5- To receive immeasurable rewards, we must endure the hardship of waking up before dawn. «تتجافى - اُخفى لهم»

6- Sa'di, although you are eloquent and a counselor; success comes through action, not through eloquence. «بما كانوا یعملون»

Ayah

أَفَمَن كَانَ مُؤْمِنًا كَمَن كَانَ فَاسِقًا ۚ لَّا يَسْتَوُۥنَ (۱۸)
Is someone who is faithful like someone who is a transgressor? They are not equal. (18)

Messages

1- When stating the truth, seek help from people's conscience by posing a question. «أفمن كان...»

2- The method of comparison is one of the best educational methods. «أفمن كان... كمن كان»

3- Not only are the disbelievers opponents of the believers, but wicked individuals (fāsiqūn) are also on the opposing front. «مؤمناً... فاسقاً»

4- Ask the people, but also declare your rightful view. «لایستون»

Ayahs

أَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ فَلَهُمْ جَنَّـٰتُ ٱلْمَأْوَىٰ نُزُلًۢا بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (۱۹)
As for those who have faith and do righteous deeds, for them will be the gardens of the Abode—a hospitality for what they used to do. (19)
وَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ فَسَقُوا۟ فَمَأْوَىٰهُمُ ٱلنَّارُ ۖ كُلَّمَآ أَرَادُوٓا۟ أَن يَخْرُجُوا۟ مِنْهَآ أُعِيدُوا۟ فِيهَا وَقِيلَ لَهُمْ ذُوقُوا۟ عَذَابَ ٱلنَّارِ ٱلَّذِى كُنتُم بِهِۦ تُكَذِّبُونَ (۲٠)
As for those who have transgressed, their refuge will be the Fire. Whenever they seek to leave it, they will be turned back into it and told: ‘Taste the punishment of the Fire which you used to deny.’ (20)

Key Points

In Ayah 18, He stated implicitly: Are the believer and the transgressor equal? In these verses, He explains the destiny of both groups in detail.

Messages

  1. Faith is not separate from action. «آمنوا و عملوا...»

  2. The believer must ensure all his deeds are righteous/praiseworthy. «الصالحات»

  3. The gardens of Paradise are for the reception/hospitality of the believers. «نُزُلاً»

  4. The transgressor who denies the Day of Judgment will remain in Hell forever. «كلّما ارادوا... اُعیدوا»

  5. The physical torment of the inhabitants of Hell is accompanied by their spiritual humiliation. «اُعیدوا فیها و قیل لهم ذوقوا»

Ayah

وَلَنُذِيقَنَّهُم مِّنَ ٱلْعَذَابِ ٱلْأَدْنَىٰ دُونَ ٱلْعَذَابِ ٱلْأَكْبَرِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ (۲۱)
We shall surely make them taste the nearer punishment prior to the greater punishment, so that they may come back. (21)

Key Points

The punishment of this world (dunya) is both near and small. The punishment of the Hereafter (akhirah) is both distant and great. According to the rule (of contrast), the word ab'ad (farther) should have been used opposite the word adna (nearer/smaller), but it cannot be said that Allah's wrath is distant. Therefore, the attribute akbar (greater) is used instead of the word ab'ad. On the other hand, it was possible for the word asghar (smaller) to be used instead of the word adna so that it would be suitable with the word akbar, but Allah's wrath cannot be considered small. Therefore, the word adna is used in one place and the word akbar in the other. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: "The meaning of al-'Adhāb al-Adnā (the nearer punishment) is the calamities, illnesses, and sufferings that a person is afflicted with in this world."(362)

362) Tafasir Nur al-Thaqalayn and Kanz al-Daqā'iq.

Messages

1- One of the Divine traditions (Sunan) is afflicting the sinner so that he may repent. «ولنذیقنّهم... لعلّهم یرجعون»

2- In upbringing (tarbiyah), love is not always effective everywhere; sometimes a harsh confrontation and wrath are necessary. «العذاب الادنى»

3- The punishment of the Hereafter is greater than all worldly punishments. «العذاب الاكبر»

4- Worldly bitterness must serve as a wake-up call and a prelude to repentance. «لعلّهم یرجعون» (The path of return is open even for transgressors.)

5- Worldly bitterness does not contradict Divine Justice, because it is based on wisdom and intended for the reform of the people. «ولنذیقنّهم... لعلّهم یرجعون»

6- In upbringing, the probability of effect is sufficient; certainty is not required. «لعلّهم یرجعون»

7- Even after tasting worldly punishments, the return of transgressors from their sins is still not compulsory. «لعلّهم یرجعون»

Ayah

وَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّن ذُكِّرَ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ رَبِّهِۦ ثُمَّ أَعْرَضَ عَنْهَآ ۚ إِنَّا مِنَ ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ مُنتَقِمُونَ (۲۲)
Who is a greater wrongdoer than him who is reminded of his Lord’s signs, whereat he disregards them? Indeed, We shall take vengeance upon the guilty. (22)

Key Points

In Ayah 15, we read that whenever the believers are reminded of the divine signs, they immediately fall into prostration. In contrast to this group, there are those whom this Ayah describes as showing inattention and turning away when reminded of the signs of Allah.

Messages

1- Turning away (i'rāḍ) from divine reminders is the greatest injustice to oneself and society. «و من اظلم»

2- Allah reminds both the believer and the non-believer, each in a specific manner. «ذُكِّروا بِایات رَبّهم خرّوا سُجداً», « ذُكِّر بِآیات رَبّه ثم اعرض»

3- The sinner's disregard for the reminder is worse than the sin itself. « ذكّروا - اعرض - اظلم»

4- Allah's attribute of being the Most Compassionate (Raḥmān) and the Most Merciful (Raḥīm) does not contradict His wrath. «انّا من المجرمین منتقمون»

5- Turning away from the divine signs is a crime, and the criminal is exposed to divine vengeance. «منتقمون»

6- Reminder and kindness are not sufficient; regarding criminals, punishment and severity are necessary. «منتقمون»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ ءَاتَيْنَا مُوسَى ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ فَلَا تَكُن فِى مِرْيَةٍ مِّن لِّقَآئِهِۦ ۖ وَجَعَلْنَـٰهُ هُدًى لِّبَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ (۲۳)
Certainly We gave Moses the Book, [declaring], ‘Do not be in doubt about the encounter with Him,’ and We made it a [source of] guidance for the Children of Israel. (23)

Key Points

Regarding what is meant by the phrase «فلاتكن فى مریة من لقائه» (So be not in doubt of His meeting), several possibilities have been suggested, including: A: Do not doubt the meeting of Prophet Moses with Allah and the reception of revelation. B: Do not doubt the meeting (reception) of the Qur'an and revelation. (Which is supported by Ayah 3 of this Surah) C: Do not doubt the meeting (encounter) of the Day of Resurrection. (Which is supported by Ayahs 10 and 14 of this Surah)

Messages

1- The prophethood of the Prophet of Islam and the descent of the heavenly Book was not an unprecedented matter. «ولقد آتینا موسى الكتاب»

2- Awareness of the history of the prophets is a source of comfort and reassurance. «ولقد آتینا موسى... فلاتكن»

3- There should be no doubt or hesitation concerning the heavenly leader. «فلاتكن فى مریة»

4- The Torah was for the guidance of the Children of Israel, not for all mankind. «هدىً لبنى اسرائیل»

Ayah

وَجَعَلْنَا مِنْهُمْ أَئِمَّةً يَهْدُونَ بِأَمْرِنَا لَمَّا صَبَرُوا۟ ۖ وَكَانُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا يُوقِنُونَ (۲٤)
When they had been patient and had conviction in Our signs, We appointed amongst them imams to guide [the people] by Our command. (24)

Messages

1- The appointment of leadership is among the divine rights. «جعلنا... ائمّة»

2- Allah determines the content, method, and scope of guidance. «یهدون بامرنا» (By the choice of Allah, not by the choice of the people)(363)

3- Certainty and patience are two necessary conditions for leadership. (The leader must have certainty regarding the goal and resist until the final stage). «صبروا... یوقنون»363) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Ayah

إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ يَفْصِلُ بَيْنَهُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ فِيمَا كَانُوا۟ فِيهِ يَخْتَلِفُونَ (۲٥)
Indeed your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning that about which they used to differ. (25)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, we read that Allah appoints worthy individuals as leaders (Rahbar) and Imams. In this Ayah, we read: Allah will settle the disputes of the people on the Day of Resurrection (Qiyamah). Perhaps the intent of the dispute is the disagreement regarding the issue of leadership, and why they showed obstinacy and contention against the divinely appointed leaders, so that Allah may hold them accountable on the Day of Resurrection. This is a type of threat for those who raised the banner of opposition against the leaders appointed by Allah.

Messages

1- Settling disputes is among the divine prerogatives (Shu'un-e Rububi) on the Day of Resurrection. «ربّك هو یفصل»

2- All disputes will be settled on the Day of Resurrection. «یفصل بینهم یوم القیامة»

3- Religious disputes are unsolvable in this world. «یفصل بینهم یوم القیامة»

4- Throughout history, human beings have always had disagreements. «كانوا فیه یختلفون»

5- Religious leaders should never become disheartened regarding their work and the guidance of the people, because disputes have always existed. «فیما كانوا فیه یختلفون»

Ayah

أَوَلَمْ يَهْدِ لَهُمْ كَمْ أَهْلَكْنَا مِن قَبْلِهِم مِّنَ ٱلْقُرُونِ يَمْشُونَ فِى مَسَـٰكِنِهِمْ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ ۖ أَفَلَا يَسْمَعُونَ (۲٦)
Does it not dawn upon them how many generations We have destroyed before them, amid [the ruins of] whose dwellings they walk? There are indeed signs in that. Will they not then listen? (26)

Key Points

«قرون» (Qurūn) is the plural of «قرن» (Qarn), meaning a people or nation that lived during the same time period. In this Ayah, which addresses the issue of the destruction of previous nations and relates to history, its message must be received through hearing. Thus, at the end of the Ayah, we read: «أفلایسمعون» (Do they not hear?). However, in the next Ayah, which speaks of barren lands becoming green, we receive the message of the Ayah through seeing. Thus, at the end of that Ayah, we read: «أفلایبصرون» (Do they not see?).

Messages

1- The people of Mecca were aware of the extinction of previous nations, and the remnants of those nations were along their pathways, yet they did not take heed. «أو لم یهد لهم»

2- Historical transformations are governed by law. (The destruction of previous nations on the one hand, and the command for others to take heed on the other, indicates that historical events are not accidental; rather, they hold secrets and wisdom that must be understood.) «أو لم یهد لهم»

3- The downfall of rebellious tribes is among the best sources of guidance for future generations. «أو لم یهد لهم كم اهلكنا من قبلهم»

4- Rebellious and destroyed tribes in history are numerous. «كم اهلكنا... من القرون»

5- Visiting ancient ruins and the remnants left by previous nations is recommended in Islam, provided that it is accompanied by admonition and taking heed, not by heedlessness. «أولم یهد لهم - یمشون فى مساكنهم»

6- The best scenes for taking heed are those where the person is physically present. «یمشون فى مساكنهم»

7- Preserving the ancient relics of ancestors is necessary for the admonition of future generations. «مساكنهم»

8- Investigate the factors behind the glory and humiliation of past nations and learn lessons from them. «انّ فى ذلك لعبرة»

9- The downfall of nations and civilizations is a manifestation of the Divine Signs (Ayat). «انّ ذلك لایات...»

10- If there is a listening ear, even silent ruins carry a message for us. «أفلایسمعون»

Ayah

أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ أَنَّا نَسُوقُ ٱلْمَآءَ إِلَى ٱلْأَرْضِ ٱلْجُرُزِ فَنُخْرِجُ بِهِۦ زَرْعًا تَأْكُلُ مِنْهُ أَنْعَـٰمُهُمْ وَأَنفُسُهُمْ ۖ أَفَلَا يُبْصِرُونَ (۲۷)
Do they not see that We carry water to the parched earth and with it We bring forth crops, from which they eat, themselves and their cattle? Will they not then see? (27)

Key Points

The word "juruz" refers to barren land where vegetation has ceased. (364) In the previous Ayah, the destruction of the arrogant was considered a sign of Divine power, and in this Ayah, the flourishing of the earth (is mentioned).

364) Mufradat Raghib.

Messages

1- The educational method of the Quran is to use natural transformations for the guidance of people. «أولم یروا...»

2- The movement of water-laden clouds and the rainfall in areas far from the sea is not accidental, but is by the wise Will of Allah. «انّا نسوق الماء»

3- The earth becomes fertile with water «فنخرج به زرعاً», but some hearts do not become fertile despite all those instructive signs! «أولم یهد لهم»

4- In material utilization, animals precede humans. «انعامهم و انفسهم» (Animals eat more, eat more easily, and do not have to cook, wash, or peel!)

5- If man lacks insight, it is better that his name be mentioned after the livestock. «انعامهم و انفسهم أفلایبصرون»

6- Do not overlook the system of rainfall and the growth of plants. «أفلایبصرون»

Ayahs

وَيَقُولُونَ مَتَىٰ هَـٰذَا ٱلْفَتْحُ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ (۲۸)
They say, ‘When will this judgement be, if you are truthful?’ (28)
قُلْ يَوْمَ ٱلْفَتْحِ لَا يَنفَعُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ إِيمَـٰنُهُمْ وَلَا هُمْ يُنظَرُونَ (۲۹)
Say, ‘On the day of judgement their [newly found] faith will not avail the faithless, nor will they be granted any respite.’ (29)
فَأَعْرِضْ عَنْهُمْ وَٱنتَظِرْ إِنَّهُم مُّنتَظِرُونَ (۳٠)
So turn away from them, and wait. They too are waiting. (30)

Key Points

In the previous verses, Allah stated: We will take vengeance upon the criminals and punish them. The disbelievers mockingly ask: When is the day of your victory, O believers, and the day of vengeance upon us? Allah responds to this action and their speech as well.

Messages

1- We should respond to the statements of opponents. «یقولون... قل»

2- Faith is of no benefit at the moment of distress (or compulsion). «لاینفع... ایمانهم»

3- Although granting respite is one of the Divine traditions, «امهلهم رویدا»(365) it also has limits. «و لا هم ینظرون»

4- After argumentation and warning, the final stage is turning away from the deviants. «فاعرض عنهم»

5- The passage of time clarifies the issues and differences between the followers of truth and falsehood. «وانتظر»

6- The leader must be optimistic and encouraged about the future. «وانتظر»

7- The disbelievers are always awaiting the defeat of the Muslims. «انّهم منتظرون»365) At-Tariq, 17.
